headline: Ciena stock slides, Trade Desk jumps on OpenAI ad talks
summary: Yahoo Finance Markets and Data Editor Jared Blikre joins Markets Catalysts Host Julie Hyman to track several of the day's top trending stock tickers. Ciena (CIEN) stock drops despite reporting strong earnings, as investors react to guidance after a big run-up in the stock. Trade Desk (TTD) jumps on reports it may work with OpenAI (OPAI.PVT) to sell ads on ChatGPT. Kroger (KR) is also in focus after giving a softer profit outlook amid rising grocery competition. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Catalysts.

content.title: Ciena stock slides, Trade Desk jumps on OpenAI ad talks

Trade Desk (TTD) jumps on reports it may work with OpenAI (OPAI.PVT) to sell ads on ChatGPT. Kroger (KR) is also in focus after giving a softer profit outlook amid rising grocery competition.

To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Catalysts.

headline: Back From the Dead: The Trade Desk Soars as Hope Is Resurrected
summary: The Trade Desk (NASDAQ:TTD) had been largely left for dead by Wall Street investors over the past year. The ad-tech pioneer, once a high-flying darling of the programmatic advertising space, shed some 60% of its value as Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) increasingly encroached on its market share in retail media and connected TV (CTV). Slowing growth and ... Back From the Dead: The Trade Desk Soars as Hope Is Resurrected

headline: Software Stocks Are Roaring Back. They're Crushing Chips.
summary: On the flip side, the iShares Semiconductor ETF, or SOXX, fell 2.8% following a Bloomberg report that said the U.S. was mulling requiring American approval for AI chip shipments around the world.  The IGV ETF is beating the SOXX ETF by 12.4 percentage points this week, which would be its largest weekly outperformance based on data going back to July 2001, according to Dow Jones Market Data.  It's beating the SOXX by 17.3 percentage points over the past six trading days, which would be its largest six-day outperformance on record, also based on data going back to July 2001.

content.title: Software Stocks Are Roaring Back. They're Crushing Chips.
content.summary: On the flip side, the iShares Semiconductor ETF, or SOXX, fell 2.8% following a Bloomberg report that said the U.S. was mulling requiring American approval for AI chip shipments around the world.  The IGV ETF is beating the SOXX ETF by 12.4 percentage points this week, which would be its largest weekly outperformance based on data going back to July 2001, according to Dow Jones Market Data.  It's beating the SOXX by 17.3 percentage points over the past six trading days, which would be its largest six-day outperformance on record, also based on data going back to July 2001.

headline: Is The Trade Desk, Inc. (TTD) A Good Stock To Buy?
summary: We came across a bullish thesis on The Trade Desk, Inc. on Compounding Your Wealth’s Substack by Sergey. In this article, we will summarize the bulls’ thesis on TTD. The Trade Desk, Inc.’s share was trading at $25.17 as of March 4th. TTD’s trailing and forward P/E were 17 and 14 respectively according to Yahoo Finance. The Trade Desk (TTD) is a leading independent programmatic advertising platform, offering AI-driven tools to optimize digital content delivery across […]

headline: Why The Trade Desk (TTD) Story Is Being Rewritten As Growth And Valuation Expectations Reset
summary: Trade Desk’s fair value estimate has been cut sharply from US$53.94 to US$32.95, putting the reset in expectations front and center for investors. This change lines up with a more cautious tone from Street research, where many firms are lowering price targets and reassessing growth assumptions after Q4 results and softer Q1 guidance. As you read on, you will see how this evolving narrative is taking shape and what to watch to stay on top of it. Stay updated as the Fair Value for Trade Desk...
